roles.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist - Bayesian Experimentation & Causal Inference
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional fit on core craft—this role is explicitly designed around causal inference and Bayesian experimentation, perfectly matching your expertise and player-coach profile at Staff/Principal level. Healthcare marketplace (mental health access) aligns with values; Series D growth stage with clear 0-to-1 opportunities (building "confidence ladder," institionalizing standards). Main concern: compensation appears to max ~$25K (likely $250K total), which is at the lower edge of your $230-260K target; AI/ML is not a primary focus of this role, representing a growth edge that won't be developed here.

roles.title: Staff Statistics Engineer - Feature Flagging and Experimentation
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional match on core craft—this role is literally building the experimentation/feature-flagging infrastructure the candidate specializes in, with stated need for CUPED, sequential analysis, and causal inference expertise. Staff-level player-coach role with 0-to-1 building on new products aligns perfectly with track and stage preference. Datadog's scale (30k+ customers, $8k+ employees, public company) and product analytics/APM expansion signal strong builder culture and direct impact. Comp trajectory is realistic ($230-260K fits Datadog's disclosed range and Staff tier), and domain (developer tools, observability, no ads/defense/crypto) is ideal. Growth edge in AI/LLM is less central to this role than ideal, but LLM observability is emerging in Datadog's stack. Main consideration: Datadog is Series D+ (post-IPO), slightly later-stage than stated sweet spot, though the *products* are 0-to-1.

roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Strong alignment on seniority (Director-level leadership role), domain (healthcare marketplace, not ads/defense/crypto), and core craft (cohort analysis, experimentation, funnel optimization). Series D stage fits growth preference. Key concerns: (1) compensation data suggests $220-250K range, toward lower end of target; (2) role emphasizes patient acquisition/retention analytics over causal inference infrastructure—more analytics-driven than experimentation platform building; (3) limited signals on AI/ML product development (growth edge). This is a high-impact leadership role in a mission-driven consumer health business, but may feel more "analytics leadership" than "hands-on builder of advanced experimentation systems."

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist - Generative AI
roles.fit_notes: Strong technical alignment: Roblox explicitly emphasizes experimentation, causal inference, and GenAI product evaluation—core to your expertise. The role offers deep AI/ML work with LLM applications and a 0-to-1 mentality around creator tools and code intelligence. Key concerns: Roblox is post-Series D (IPO'd 2021), larger than your Series B-D preference, which may dilute hands-on impact despite IC-leaning positioning. Compensation is significantly above target ($289-497K range), mitigating risk. Domain fit is strong (gaming/creator platform), though scale and organizational maturity may feel less startup-like than ideal.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment: the role explicitly centers experimentation, causal inference, A/B testing, and mentorship—directly matching the candidate's core expertise and player-coach profile. Domain fit is strong (restaurants/SMB commerce, not ads/defense/crypto). Primary concerns: (1) Toast appears to be Series C+ (well-funded, public infrastructure play), not the Series B-D sweet spot; (2) compensation baseline unclear but likely achievable at $230-260K given Staff level; (3) AI/ML relevance is present (menu recommendation, demand forecasting) but secondary to statistical modeling and ML Ops rigor rather than cutting-edge LLM work. Spotify background translates well to large-scale personalization and experimentation culture.

roles.title: Senior Data Science Manager, Customer Experience
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Strong domain and seniority fit: Headway is a mission-driven Series D healthcare company (perfect domain match—avoids excluded sectors) seeking a Staff-level player-coach to lead a data science team in customer experience. The role emphasizes experimentation, measurement frameworks, and AI integration, directly leveraging your core expertise. Key concern: compensation trajectory is unclear—levels.fyi shows $22-25K (likely incomplete/outdated data), making it hard to validate whether Headway can reach your $230-260K target; you'll need to clarify total comp during conversations. The CX focus is slightly narrower than your preferred 0-to-1 platform work, but the measurement and AI components offer meaningful growth edges.

Core concern: craft match is softer than ideal. The role emphasizes customer support optimization, sentiment analysis, and performance management rather than experimentation platforms or causal inference infrastructure. AI relevance is present but secondary to measurement frameworks. Compensation trajectory is reasonable for Series D NYC but slightly below target given no disclosed range.

roles.title: Head of Data Science - Monetization
roles.fit_notes: Nextdoor offers a strong seniority match (Director/Senior Manager level with player-coach expectations) and solid craft fit around experimentation/measurement frameworks, but the monetization focus (ads platform) conflicts with the candidate's stated aversion to ads-tech. The role emphasizes platform health and neighbor experience, which softens the ads concern somewhat, but this remains a material misalignment. Comp is realistic ($230-260K lands well within levels.fyi range); culture signals are positive (semi-embedded model, direct impact, team building); but limited AI/ML relevance and Series D+ scale (not growth-stage 0-to-1 building) reduce overall fit.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist - Product Analytics
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft and seniority alignment: this Staff-level role is built for someone with deep experimentation and causal inference expertise who can set measurement strategy and design standards across teams—exactly the candidate's sweet spot. Domain fit is excellent (healthcare, not ads/crypto/defense), and the mission-driven builder culture aligns well. Main concerns: (1) Series D is late-stage growth, not the 0-to-1 preference, though measurement infrastructure likely still has greenfield opportunity; (2) AI/ML is not core to this role (causal inference/experimentation focus instead); (3) comp appears to max ~$250K base + equity, which fits target range but may run light depending on equity vesting/refresh—levels.fyi data suggests $220-250K band, so upper end of candidate's range is achievable but not guaranteed.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist - Product
roles.location: San Francisco, CA - Hybrid; New York, NY - Hybrid;
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment—this is a textbook Staff-level experimentation/causal inference role that directly matches the candidate's core expertise and player-coach profile. Gusto is a strong cultural fit (builder mindset, product impact focus, quality team signals) in a trusted domain (SMB fintech, not ads/crypto/defense). Compensation at $257-283K exceeds the $230-260K target. Main concerns: Gusto is post-Series D/growth-stage rather than true 0-to-1 Series B-D, and AI/LLM work appears secondary to core analytics rigor—this is more "AI-assisted" than AI-product-led.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist - Marketing Analytics
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment: this is a genuine Staff-level player-coach role centered on causal inference (geo tests, holdouts, quasi-experimental design) and incrementality measurement—the candidate's core expertise. Strong domain fit (healthcare, mission-driven, consumer-facing). Growth-stage company (Series D, 60k+ providers, 1M+ patients) with real 0-to-1 measurement system building ahead. Key concerns: (1) Limited AI/ML product work (marketing analytics focus, not generative AI), which is a stated growth edge. (2) Comp trajectory unclear—levels.fyi shows $22-25k (likely outdated or incomplete data), and Series D healthcare typically pays $200-250k base+bonus; needs verification against $230-260k target. (3) Healthcare regulation/compliance adds complexity vs. pure consumer tech. Strong fit on craft, culture, and impact; verify comp and expectations on AI/ML involvement.

Compensation is excellent ($289-497K well above target). However, Roblox is public/mature (not Series B-D growth stage), and the role appears individual-contributor focused rather than player-coach/leadership track. The domain (gaming/creator platform) is a good fit, but growth-stage preference and strategic leadership aspirations may not be fully realized here.

roles.title: Director of Data Science, Ads Measurement & Attribution
roles.location: San Francisco, CA, US; Seattle, WA, US
roles.fit_notes: This is a technically excellent fit on core expertise—causal inference, A/B testing, experiment design, and measurement science are central to the role. Seniority and leadership scope (Director, team-building, strategy) align well with player-coach expectations. However, the role is heavily ads-focused (ads measurement, advertiser ROI, attribution for ad campaigns), which directly conflicts with the candidate's stated avoidance of ads-tech. Pinterest is also mature/public (Series L+), not growth-stage, and the AI/ML product angle is minimal. This is a 'brilliant fit on hard skills, wrong company/domain' situation.

roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment: the role explicitly demands causal inference, metrics definition, and strategic influence—core strengths from Spotify experimentation platform work. Compensation trajectory is outstanding ($289-497K range significantly exceeds $230-260K target). Creator ecosystem focus aligns with consumer/marketplace values and avoids blocked domains. Main concern is stage fit: Roblox is a mature public company (not Series B-D growth stage), which may reduce the 0-to-1 building appeal and increase organizational complexity. AI/ML component is present but positioned as supporting tool rather than core product innovation.

roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment—this Principal role heavily emphasizes experimentation, causal inference, and statistical rigor in a quantitative-focused org. Compensation is strong ($289-497K range well exceeds $230-260K target). However, safety/content moderation is a defensive rather than growth-oriented domain, and Roblox is mature/public (not Series B-D), which somewhat conflicts with the candidate's 0-to-1 building preference. The role offers deep technical impact and team influence but may feel less like greenfield innovation.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist, Forecasting in Monetization
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority alignment—this is a world-class forecasting + experimentation role perfectly matched to the candidate's core expertise in causal inference, A/B testing, and subscription dynamics. Duolingo's mission-driven culture, builder mentality, and direct executive impact align well with values. Critical blocker: compensation is severely misaligned. Duolingo's posted range ($204-392K) appears garbled in data, but public benchmarks suggest Staff DS roles there trend $250-350K base+equity—which should land in target range when properly structured. The role is also less growth-stage (Series C/D, profitable, 500M+ users) and lacks AI/ML product development focus, making it a lateral move rather than upward skew on the candidate's growth edge.
roles.description:
<p>Our mission at Duolingo is to develop the best education in the world and make it universally available. It’s a big mission, and that’s where you come in!</p>
<p>At Duolingo, you’ll join a team that cares about educating our users, experimenting with big ideas, making fact-based decisions, and finding innovative solutions to complex problems. You’ll have limitless learning opportunities and daily collaborations with world-class minds — while doing work that’s both meaningful

roles.title: Data Scientist, User Operations
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and AI relevance match—this role is built around experimentation, causal inference, and metrics design, perfectly aligned with your core expertise. The work is deeply impactful (human-AI collaboration at scale) and at a frontier company. Major concerns: (1) Seniority framing suggests individual contributor depth over player-coach/team leadership (explicit 'own' vs. lead), (2) Stage fit is diluted—OpenAI is Series C+ scale-up, not 0-to-1 Series B-D growth stage, and (3) Comp is likely $300-400K+ (levels.fyi data incomplete), which overshoots your target and may indicate overleveling or misalignment on role scope.
roles.description:
About the Team

OpenAI’s User Operations organization is building the data and intelligence layer behind AI-assisted operations — the systems that decide when automation should help users, when humans should step in, and how both improve over time. Our flagship platform is transforming customer support into a model for “agent-first” operations across OpenAI.

About the Role

As a Data Scientist on User Operations, you’ll design the models, metrics, and experimentation frameworks that power OpenA

roles.title: Principal Data Scientist – Product
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft and seniority alignment: Principal-level role with deep experimentation/causal inference work and player-coach responsibilities is a near-perfect match for candidate's core expertise and career trajectory. Compensation ($257-283K base, likely $270-290K+ total) exceeds target range. However, Gusto is Series D+ mature growth/public-adjacent (not 0-to-1 building), and the role leans heavily toward strategic leadership/execution rather than hands-on technical depth—candidate may find it 60/40 or 70/30 strategic rather than the preferred 50/50 split. Domain fit is strong (small business SaaS, not ads/defense/crypto), and AI/ML product development opportunity exists but isn't core to the role description.
